footer.about:
The best way to read AI-generated files on your phone.
Local-first. Zero uploads. Fully open source.
meta.description: Local-first, lightweight, opens in under a second. Markdown, HTML, JSON, YAML, TXT, CSV — files never leave your device.
hero.headline:
On your phone, read
AI-generated documents
the way they deserve
formats.title:
Six formats supported today,
all in one app
formats.sub: From ChatGPT-shared READMEs to Claude-exported research reports to JSON output from Codex — share directly, no conversion needed. More formats on the way.
fmt.md: Full GFM support: headings, lists, tables, quotes, task lists, code-block syntax highlighting, Mermaid diagrams, LaTeX math.
fmt.html: AI-generated single-file HTML, reports, dashboards, infographics — rendered in a sandboxed WebView with CSS / SVG / Canvas / local JS support.
fmt.json: Tree view, collapse/expand, key search, one-click formatting — complex API responses at a glance.
fmt.yaml: Config files, CI/CD workflows, Kubernetes manifests — collapsible groups with type hints.
fmt.txt: Pure long-form reading experience with auto section detection, adjustable font size, and fullscreen mode.
fmt.csv: Lightweight table view with horizontal scroll, column sort, and first-row field detection.
formats.foot.title: This list will keep growing
formats.foot.sub: More formats (DOCX, EPUB, RTF, …) are under evaluation. File an issue on GitHub to tell us which one you want next.
privacy.title:
Your files,
never leave your device
privacy.sub: All rendering, parsing and caching happens locally on the device. Offline by default. Zero uploads, zero accounts, zero tracking. We never see your document content, and we don't ask you to sign up.
privacy.l1.p: Parsing runs inside the system WebView on your device — never touches any external server.
privacy.l2.p: Dangerous network access is blocked by default. JS execution is opt-in per document — you control every HTML file.
privacy.l3.p: The local database stores only filename, path, type, and last-opened time. Document text is never persisted.
privacy.l4.p: No analytics SDKs, no ad networks, no mandatory accounts. Just open and read.
feat.title:
Immersive like Kindle,
clean like Safari Reader
feat.sub: Built for long-form reading — no notifications, no interruptions, no injected ads. You stay in control.
f1.p: Follows system, or lock independently. Tuned contrast and color temperature for long reads.
f2.p: Stepless font scaling, adjustable line height and width — remembered per document.
f3.p: Hide status and nav bars with one tap and enter true reading mode.
f4.p: Long Markdown documents get an auto-extracted H1/H2 tree, slide in from the side.
f5.p: Keyword highlight in the current doc, cross-section jumps in milliseconds.
f6.p: 100+ language grammars; horizontal scroll for long blocks; optional line numbers.
f7.p: Flow / sequence / class diagrams render inline — no need to switch to a browser.
f8.p: Inline and block-level math rendering — papers and notes read as-is.
wf.title:
From prompt to phone,
one share is all it takes
wf.sub: We don't replace ChatGPT, Claude, or Codex — we handle the last mile: making sure the files they produce actually read well on a phone.
wf.s1.d: Generate Markdown / HTML / JSON reports in chat
wf.s2.d: Send via the AI app's share sheet
wf.s3.d: Detect format · render locally · open in seconds
wf.s4.d: Read full docs on the commute, on a trip, before bed
uc.title: Designed for people who actually read AI docs
uc.sub: Not an editor. Not a knowledge base. Not a social network. Just open → render → read.
uc1.p: Have your AI produce a survey in Markdown and read it on the subway. Mermaid diagrams, code blocks, and link references all render correctly.
uc1.q: "No more bouncing between browsers just to read a report."
uc2.p: Open dashboard.html and infographic.html directly in the app — CSS, SVG, and Canvas render locally, with full offline support.
uc2.q: "The sandboxed WebView lets me open HTML from anywhere."
uc3.h: Take every chat result with you
uc3.p: Use NovaView as the default opener for every AI-generated file. Structured JSON views, scrollable CSVs, full-text search across docs.
uc3.q: "Preview.app for the AI era, on your phone."
dl.title:
Install now,
read your next AI reply immediately

doc.title: Privacy Policy
doc.sub: This policy explains how NovaView handles your data. Short answer: hardly at all — no uploads, no tracking, no mandatory account.
s1.title: 1 · Core commitment
s1.p1: Our first principle is local-first. That means:
s1.r1.v: Never leave your device; never uploaded
s1.r3.v: Not required — use it straight away
s1.p2: This policy is written in plain language. If anything is ambiguous, the contact channels in Contact take precedence.
s2.title: 2 · What we collect
s2.p1: The app itself does not collect any of your content data. We only store the following metadata locally for the "Recent files" feature:
s2.l1: Filename, extension, and file path (within the system sandbox)
s2.l3: User-set font size, theme, and dark-mode preferences
s2.p2: This data lives in an on-device SQLite database and is unrelated to your Apple ID or Google account. We cannot — and do not — access it from any server.
s2.cl.p: No file contents, no reading duration, no file paths or document fragments in crash logs, no device identifiers used for tracking.
s3.title: 3 · How files are handled
s3.p1: Files you import via Share Extension, file picker, or "Open with" follow this flow:
s3.l1: The system delivers the file; the app writes it into a sandbox temp dir (iOS Inbox / Android cacheDir)
s3.l3: Select the matching renderer: Markdown / HTML / JSON / YAML / TXT / CSV
s3.p2: The entire flow makes zero network requests. We don't, don't need to, and aren't allowed to upload your files anywhere.
s4.title: 4 · Local-first & storage
s4.p1: The local database uses SQLite and stores only metadata:
s4.p2: We never persist the text of your documents. Uninstalling the app removes all local data along with it.
s5.title: 5 · HTML sandbox
s5.p1: HTML rendering uses the platform-native WebView with these default security policies:
s5.l1: Dangerous network access blocked — cross-origin requests, cross-origin fetches, and external resources are denied by default
s5.l2: JavaScript disabled by default — users must opt in per HTML file
s5.l3: Local resource whitelist — only same-directory CSS / images / JS allowed
s5.l4: No Cookie / localStorage persistence — the WebView context is destroyed when closed
s5.p2: If an HTML file contains <script>, the app shows a clear prompt and lets you decide whether to enable it.
s6.title: 6 · Future features
s6.p1: NovaView may add features over time (more file formats, PDF annotations, AI-assisted reading, etc.). Whatever we add, we commit to these principles:
s6.l1: Local-first — offline by default; any feature that needs network access must clearly disclose it and require user opt-in
s6.l2: Opt-in — new features ship disabled and don't change the existing "open → render → read" workflow
s6.l3: Transparent docs — this policy updates alongside any new data flows
s6.l4: User-controlled — anything touching personal data can be disabled or wiped from settings
s6.cl.p: v1.4.2 contains only local file rendering and reading. No AI summarization, translation, Q&A, or any feature that uploads content or requires network calls. Release notes stay accurate.
s7.title: 7 · Third parties
s7.p1: This version integrates no third-party analytics, ad, or social SDKs.
s7.p2: App launch, file parsing, and HTML rendering all happen locally. When you download the installer, the App Store / Google Play collect a small amount of download and crash telemetry under their own policies — that data is handled by those stores, not us.
s7.p3: If we ever add optional features that call third-party services, this section will be updated first, with a change notice.
s8.title: 8 · Your rights
s8.p1: Because we don't hold your data on any server, the usual "delete account / export data" operations are trivially satisfied:
s8.l1: View local data — Settings → Data → see the recent-files list
s8.l2: Clear local data — Settings → Data → clear recents, or simply uninstall the app
s8.l3: Decline all optional data — never enable optional features, and you stay in a "zero cloud data" state
s8.p2: For any privacy questions or to exercise your rights, reach out via the contact section below.
s9.title: 9 · Security
s9.l1: All file handling happens inside the system sandbox, protected by iOS / Android sandboxing
s9.l2: The local database uses SQLite at a path only the app itself can access
s9.l3: HTML rendering uses a sandboxed WebView with cross-origin access blocked
s9.l4: App binaries are code-signed; only install from App Store / Google Play official channels
s9.l5: Client code is open source, open to community audit
s10.title: 10 · Policy changes
s10.p1: This policy may evolve with the product. When it changes we will:
s10.l1: Update the "Last updated" date at the top of this page
s10.l2: Show a change summary on next launch (for material changes)
s10.l3: Keep historical versions in the GitHub Releases page
s10.p2: We will never expand data collection without notice.
s11.title: 11 · Contact
s11.p1: Questions, suggestions, or complaints about this policy:
s11.p2: The latest version of this policy always lives at novaview.app/privacy.html. This policy is governed by the laws of the People's Republic of China and applicable regional data-protection regulations.
priv.title: Privacy Policy
priv.intro: This policy explains how NovaView handles your data. Short answer: hardly at all — no uploads, no tracking, no mandatory account.
priv.toc.title: Table of Contents
priv.s1.label: Core commitment
priv.s1.body:
Our first principle is local-first. That means:
  • File contents · Never leave your device; never uploaded · Local
  • Document parsing · Done locally inside the app · Local
  • User account · Not required — use it straight away · Disabled
  • Analytics / tracking SDK · None integrated · None
  • Ad networks · None connected · None
  • Cloud sync · Not enabled · Off

This policy is written in plain language. If anything is ambiguous, the contact channels in Contact take precedence.
priv.s2.label: What we collect
priv.s2.body:
The app itself does not collect any of your content data. We only store the following metadata locally for the "Recent files" feature:
  • Filename, extension, and file path (within the system sandbox)
  • Last opened timestamp
  • User-set font size, theme, and dark-mode preferences

This data lives in an on-device SQLite database and is unrelated to your Apple ID or Google account. We cannot — and do not — access it from any server.

📵 What we do NOT collect: No file contents, no reading duration, no file paths or document fragments in crash logs, no device identifiers used for tracking.
priv.s3.label: How files are handled
priv.s3.body:
Files you import via Share Extension, file picker, or "Open with" follow this flow:
  1. The system delivers the file; the app writes it into a sandbox temp dir (iOS Inbox / Android cacheDir)
  2. Local type detection (extension + magic number)
  3. Select the matching renderer: Markdown / HTML / JSON / YAML / TXT / CSV
  4. Render the result on screen

The entire flow makes zero network requests. We don't, don't need to, and aren't allowed to upload your files anywhere.
priv.s4.label: Local-first & storage
priv.s4.body:
The local database uses SQLite and stores only metadata:
  • id · primary key
  • filename · filename
  • path · system sandbox path
  • type · file type
  • last_open_time · last opened time
  • metadata · user-set font size, theme, etc.

We never persist the text of your documents. Uninstalling the app removes all local data along with it.
priv.s5.label: HTML sandbox
priv.s5.body:
HTML rendering uses the platform-native WebView with these default security policies:
  • Dangerous network access blocked — cross-origin requests, cross-origin fetches, and external resources are denied by default
  • JavaScript disabled by default — users must opt in per HTML file
  • Local resource whitelist — only same-directory CSS / images / JS allowed
  • No Cookie / localStorage persistence — the WebView context is destroyed when closed

If an HTML file contains <script>, the app shows a clear prompt and lets you decide whether to enable it.
priv.s6.label: Future features
priv.s6.body:
NovaView may add features over time (more file formats, PDF annotations, AI-assisted reading, etc.). Whatever we add, we commit to these principles:
  • Local-first — offline by default; any feature that needs network access must clearly disclose it and require user opt-in
  • Opt-in — new features ship disabled and don't change the existing "open → render → read" workflow
  • Transparent docs — this policy updates alongside any new data flows
  • User-controlled — anything touching personal data can be disabled or wiped from settings

🔭 What this version does: v1.4.2 contains only local file rendering and reading. No AI summarization, translation, Q&A, or any feature that uploads content or requires network calls. Release notes stay accurate.
priv.s7.label: Third parties
priv.s7.body:
This version integrates no third-party analytics, ad, or social SDKs.

App launch, file parsing, and HTML rendering all happen locally. When you download the installer, the App Store / Google Play collect a small amount of download and crash telemetry under their own policies — that data is handled by those stores, not us.

If we ever add optional features that call third-party services, this section will be updated first, with a change notice.
priv.s8.label: Your rights
priv.s8.body:
Because we don't hold your data on any server, the usual "delete account / export data" operations are trivially satisfied:
  • View local data — Settings → Data → see the recent-files list
  • Clear local data — Settings → Data → clear recents, or simply uninstall the app
  • Decline all optional data — never enable optional features, and you stay in a "zero cloud data" state

For any privacy questions or to exercise your rights, reach out via the contact section below.
priv.s9.label: Security
priv.s9.body:
Security practices we follow:
  • All file handling happens inside the system sandbox, protected by iOS / Android sandboxing
  • The local database uses SQLite at a path only the app itself can access
  • HTML rendering uses a sandboxed WebView with cross-origin access blocked
  • App binaries are code-signed; only install from App Store / Google Play official channels
  • Client code is open source, open to community audit

If you find a security issue, contact us at security@novaview.app; we respond within 72 hours.
priv.s10.label: Policy changes
priv.s10.body:
This policy may evolve with the product. When it changes we will:
  • Update the "Last updated" date at the top of this page
  • Show a change summary on next launch (for material changes)
  • Keep historical versions in the GitHub Releases page

We will never expand data collection without notice.
priv.s11.label: Contact
priv.s11.body:
Questions, suggestions, or complaints about this policy:
  • Email · privacy@novaview.app
  • Security · security@novaview.app
  • GitHub · github.com/Woods30/NovaView

The latest version of this policy always lives at novaview.app/privacy.html. This policy is governed by the laws of the People's Republic of China and applicable regional data-protection regulations.
